item.title: Toward A Public Science of Model Behavior
item.description: Our vision for an open scientific ecosystem for measuring model behaviors

item.title: Scalably Extracting Latent Representations of Users
item.description: Constructing datasets and training decoders to extract user models from language models

item.title: Language Model Circuits Are Sparse in the Neuron Basis
item.description: A new technique for tracing sparse and faithful circuits directly on a model's MLPs

item.title: Monitoring SWE-bench Agents
item.description: Partnering with SWE-bench to enable reliable monitoring of AI coding agents

item.title: Training Language Models to Explain Their Own Computations
item.description: We trained explainer models to verbalize the content of AI systems' internal computations

item.title: Automatically Jailbreaking Frontier Language Models with Investigator Agents
item.description: Discovering cost-effective attacks with reinforcement learning

item.title: Surfacing Pathological Behaviors in Language Models
item.description: Improving our investigator agents with propensity bounds

item.title: Investigating Truthfulness in a Pre-Release o3 Model
item.description: o3 frequently fabricates actions it took to fulfill user requests, and elaborately justifies the fabrications when confronted

item.title: Introducing Docent
item.description: A system for analyzing and intervening on agent behavior

item.title: Monitor: An AI-Driven Observability Interface
item.description: An interface designed to help humans observe, understand, and steer computations inside models

item.title: Eliciting Language Model Behaviors with Investigator Agents
item.description: Language models trained to automatically surface harmful behaviors in language models

item.title: Scaling Automatic Neuron Explanation
item.description: Open-source AI systems trained to describe components of other AI systems at the level of a human expert
